title: Door Lock Fault
shortDescription: The door lock system has detected a fault - the door cannot be verified as locked.
fullDescription: Error code F5E2 on Kenmore washers (typically Whirlpool-manufactured models) indicates the door lock assembly has not confirmed the door is securely locked within the expected time. The washer will not begin a cycle without confirmed door lock for safety during high-speed spin.

causes.cause: Wiring fault between lock and control board
Main control board door lock circuit failure

symptoms: F5 E2 displayed on control panel
Door does not lock when cycle starts
Washer will not start any cycle
Clicking noise from door area without engagement

fixSteps.title: Close door firmly
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door completely and close it firmly until you hear the latch click. Ensure no clothing is caught in the door seal.

fixSteps.title: Inspect door strike and latch
fixSteps.instruction: Examine the door strike plate on the washer frame and the latch on the door. Check for damage, wear, or misalignment.

fixSteps.title: Test door lock assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer. Access the door lock assembly behind the door boot seal. Disconnect the wiring and test the lock switch contacts with a multimeter.

fixSteps.title: Replace door lock assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the door lock mounting screws. Disconnect the wire harness. Install the new lock assembly, reconnect, and test.

whenToCallPro: If the door lock and wiring test good but the error persists, the control board may need professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Kenmore washer F5 E2 error door lock fix

faq.q: Which manufacturer made my Kenmore washer?
faq.a: Kenmore washers are made by various manufacturers. Models starting with 110 are typically Whirlpool, 796 are LG, and some are Samsung. The error codes vary by manufacturer.

faq.q: Can I bypass the door lock?
faq.a: Never bypass the door lock. It prevents the door from opening during high-speed spin, which could cause serious injury.

faq.q: Is the door lock the same part as a Whirlpool washer?
faq.a: If your Kenmore model starts with 110, it is likely Whirlpool-manufactured and uses Whirlpool parts. Check the model number cross-reference.

title: Motor Speed Sensing Error
shortDescription: The motor speed sensor is not detecting proper motor rotation.
fullDescription: Error code F7E1 indicates the motor speed sensor (tachometer or Hall sensor) is not detecting proper motor rotation. The control board commands the motor to run but does not receive the expected speed feedback signal. This can indicate a mechanical blockage, motor failure, or sensor issue.

causes.cause: Object jammed between drum and tub

symptoms: F7 E1 on display
Drum does not spin or agitate
Motor hums but drum does not move
Error appears during wash or spin cycle

fixSteps.title: Reduce load
fixSteps.instruction: Open the washer and remove excess items. An overloaded drum can prevent the motor from reaching its target speed.

fixSteps.title: Check drum rotation
fixSteps.instruction: With the washer unplugged, try to rotate the drum by hand. It should turn freely with slight resistance. If stuck, check for foreign objects between the inner drum and outer tub.

fixSteps.title: Inspect motor connections
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer. Access the motor (usually from the back). Check the wiring harness connector to the motor and speed sensor for looseness or corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Test motor and sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Test the motor stator windings for proper resistance. Check the rotor position sensor (RPS) or Hall sensor for proper resistance and signal output.

whenToCallPro: If the motor needs replacement, or if the stator and rotor show signs of damage.
videoSearchQuery: Kenmore washer F7 E1 error motor speed sensor fix

faq.q: Can a belt cause F7E1?
faq.a: Many newer Kenmore washers use a direct-drive motor without a belt. If your model has a belt, a broken or slipping belt could cause F7E1.

faq.q: What is a motor rotor position sensor?
faq.a: The RPS is a sensor on the motor stator that detects the position and speed of the rotor magnets. It provides feedback for the motor controller to properly drive the motor.

faq.q: Is the motor expensive to replace?
faq.a: Direct-drive washer motors consist of a stator and rotor. Replacing just the failed component (sensor, stator, or rotor) is cheaper than replacing the entire motor assembly.

title: Low Water Flow / Fill Error
shortDescription: The washer is not receiving enough water to fill properly.
fullDescription: Error code F8E1 (also shown as LO FL on some models) indicates the washer detected insufficient water flow. The water level did not reach the minimum required amount within the fill time limit. This is commonly a water supply issue.

causes.cause: Water supply faucets not fully open

symptoms: F8 E1 or LO FL displayed
Washer stops shortly after starting
Little or no water in drum
Cycle will not advance past fill stage

fixSteps.title: Verify water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Check that both hot and cold water faucets behind the washer are fully open.

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet screens
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the water. Disconnect the hoses from the back of the washer. Remove and clean the mesh filter screens inside the valve inlets.

fixSteps.title: Check hoses for kinks
fixSteps.instruction: Straighten any kinks in the supply hoses. Replace hoses that are old, cracked, or permanently kinked.

fixSteps.title: Test inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Measure the inlet valve solenoid coils with a multimeter. Each should read 500-1500 ohms. Replace the valve if readings are open or shorted.

whenToCallPro: If water pressure is confirmed adequate and the inlet valve needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Kenmore washer F8 E1 LO FL error water fill fix

faq.q: What does LO FL mean?
faq.a: LO FL stands for Low Flow and is an alternate display for the F8E1 error. Both mean the same thing - insufficient water filling.

faq.q: Can frozen pipes cause F8E1?
faq.a: Yes, in winter, frozen supply pipes will prevent water from reaching the washer. Thaw the pipes before using the washer.

faq.q: Should both hot and cold be connected?
faq.a: Yes. Most Kenmore washers expect both hot and cold connections. If only one is connected, certain cycles may trigger F8E1.

title: Unbalanced Load
shortDescription: The washer has detected an unbalanced load during the spin cycle.
fullDescription: Error code UE (on LG-manufactured Kenmore models, typically 796.xxxxx series) indicates the washer detected an unbalanced load during the spin cycle. The washer attempted to redistribute the load by reversing and adjusting the drum speed multiple times but could not achieve adequate balance for high-speed spin.

causes.cause: Single large or heavy item creating imbalance
Too few items in the drum
Worn shock absorbers or suspension springs

symptoms: UE or uE displayed during spin cycle
Washer stops and will not spin
Excessive vibration before error
Loud banging or thumping during spin attempt
Clothes still wet at end of cycle

fixSteps.title: Redistribute the load
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door and redistribute clothes evenly around the drum. Mix heavy items (jeans, towels) with lighter items for balance.

fixSteps.title: Add or remove items
fixSteps.instruction: If washing a single heavy item (blanket, comforter), add a few towels to help balance the load. If the drum is stuffed, remove some items.

fixSteps.title: Level the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Use a bubble level on top of the washer. Adjust the leveling feet until the washer is level front-to-back and side-to-side. Tighten the lock nuts.

fixSteps.title: Check suspension system
fixSteps.instruction: If UE occurs frequently with properly distributed loads, check the shock absorbers or suspension springs. Push down on the drum and release - it should bounce once and settle, not keep bouncing.

whenToCallPro: If the suspension system components are worn and need replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Kenmore LG washer UE error unbalanced load fix

faq.q: What is the difference between uE and UE?
faq.a: Lowercase uE means the washer is attempting to rebalance. Uppercase UE means the rebalancing attempts failed and the cycle stopped.

faq.q: Can one heavy item cause UE?
faq.a: Yes. A single heavy item like a comforter or rug creates severe imbalance. Add towels to distribute the weight more evenly.

faq.q: My washer gets UE on every load. What is wrong?
faq.a: If UE occurs regardless of load size and distribution, the suspension system (shock absorbers or springs) is likely worn and needs replacement.

title: Drain Error
shortDescription: The washer cannot drain the water within the allowed time.
fullDescription: Error code OE (on LG-manufactured Kenmore models) indicates the washer could not drain the water within the time limit. The drain pump ran but the water level did not drop sufficiently. This is typically caused by a clogged drain filter, blocked drain hose, or a failed drain pump.
symptoms: OE error code on display
Water remaining in drum after cycle
Clothes soaking wet
Washer stops during drain phase
Drain pump may buzz or hum

fixSteps.title: Clean drain pump filter
fixSteps.instruction: Open the small access door at the lower front of the washer. Place a shallow pan and towels underneath. Slowly open the drain filter cap and let water drain. Remove debris from the filter.

fixSteps.title: Check drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the washer out and check the drain hose for kinks. Disconnect from the standpipe and check for internal blockages.

fixSteps.title: Inspect pump impeller
fixSteps.instruction: After removing the filter, look into the pump housing with a flashlight. Check for small objects (coins, pins, buttons) lodged in the impeller.

fixSteps.title: Test drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: If the pump is not running at all, test the motor with a multimeter. If running but not pumping, the impeller may be broken.

whenToCallPro: If the drain pump motor has failed and needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Kenmore LG washer OE error drain fix

faq.q: How do I manually drain the washer?
faq.a: Open the drain filter access door at the bottom front. Place a shallow pan underneath and slowly unscrew the filter cap. Water will flow out slowly.

faq.q: How often should I clean the drain filter?
faq.a: Clean the drain filter every 1-2 months, or more often if you wash pet blankets or heavily soiled items.

faq.q: Can coins damage the drain pump?
faq.a: Yes. Coins and other hard objects can jam or break the drain pump impeller blades, requiring pump replacement.

title: Water Inlet Error
shortDescription: The washer is not filling with water or the water level sensor detects a problem.
fullDescription: Error code IE (sometimes displayed as 1E due to font similarity) on LG-manufactured Kenmore washers indicates a water inlet problem. Either the water is not entering the washer or the water level sensor (pressure switch) is not detecting proper fill.

causes.cause: Water supply faucets closed or partially closed
Main control board water level circuit failure

symptoms: IE displayed on control panel
Washer does not fill with water
Cycle does not start or stops early
Long delay before error appears

fixSteps.title: Check water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure both hot and cold water faucets are fully open. Turn them off and back on.

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet screens
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off water. Disconnect hoses from washer. Clean the mesh filter screens in the valve inlets.

fixSteps.title: Test water inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance of each inlet valve solenoid coil. Expected range is 300-1500 ohms. Replace if open.

fixSteps.title: Check pressure switch
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the pressure switch hose for kinks or blockages. Blow gently through the hose. Test the pressure switch for proper operation.

whenToCallPro: If the inlet valve and pressure switch both test good, the control board may need professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Kenmore LG washer IE error water inlet fix

faq.q: Is IE the same as 1E?
faq.a: Yes. The display font makes uppercase I look like the number 1. Both refer to the same inlet water error.

faq.q: Can low water pressure cause IE?
faq.a: Yes. The washer needs at least 20 PSI water pressure to fill properly. Low pressure causes slow filling and can trigger IE.

faq.q: Does IE mean the sensor is bad?
faq.a: Not necessarily. IE can be caused by the water supply, inlet valve, or the pressure sensor. Start with the simplest checks first.

title: Door Open Error
shortDescription: The washer detects the door is open or cannot confirm the door is closed.
fullDescription: Error code dE on LG-manufactured Kenmore washers indicates the door switch does not confirm the door is closed. This could be a simple matter of the door not being fully latched, a faulty door switch, or a damaged door latch mechanism.
symptoms: dE displayed on control panel
Washer will not start
Door appears closed but washer does not recognize it
May hear clicking from door area

fixSteps.title: Close door firmly
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door fully and close it again, pushing firmly until you feel and hear the latch click into place.

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Look around the entire door seal perimeter for clothing, small items, or debris preventing full closure.

fixSteps.title: Inspect door switch
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer. Access the door switch (usually behind the front panel or in the door frame). Test the switch with a multimeter for continuity when the latch engages.

fixSteps.title: Replace door switch or latch
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the door switch assembly if it does not change state when the door is latched. Check the latch hook for damage while the switch is accessible.

whenToCallPro: Only if the switch and latch both test good, indicating a possible control board issue.
videoSearchQuery: Kenmore LG washer dE error door open fix

faq.q: Can I open the door during a cycle?
faq.a: Most front-load washers lock the door once a cycle starts. Some models allow pausing and opening during the wash phase but not during spin.

faq.q: Is the door switch expensive?
faq.a: Door switches are typically inexpensive ($10-$30) and relatively easy to replace.

faq.q: Can children cause dE errors?
faq.a: If children play with the washer door, it may not close properly. Use the child lock feature to prevent tampering.

title: Locked Motor Error
shortDescription: The drive motor is locked or overloaded and cannot rotate.
fullDescription: Error code LE on LG-manufactured Kenmore washers indicates the drive motor is electrically overloaded or mechanically locked. The motor control detected excessive current draw, indicating the motor cannot rotate freely. This can be caused by overloading, a mechanical jam, or a motor failure.

causes.cause: Object jammed in drum or pump
Failed stator or rotor in motor

symptoms: LE displayed on control panel
Drum does not rotate at all
Motor may hum without moving
Error may appear at start of cycle or during agitation

fixSteps.title: Reset and reduce load
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer for 30 minutes (this resets the motor thermal protection). Remove some items to reduce the load.

fixSteps.title: Check drum rotation
fixSteps.instruction: Try spinning the drum by hand. If it does not rotate freely, something is mechanically jammed.

fixSteps.title: Inspect stator and rotor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the back panel to access the motor. Check for loose magnets on the rotor. Inspect the stator for burn marks or damaged windings.

fixSteps.title: Test motor components
fixSteps.instruction: Measure the stator winding resistance between each pair of leads. All readings should be equal. Check the rotor position sensor for proper resistance.

whenToCallPro: If the motor stator or rotor needs replacement, or if bearings are seized.
videoSearchQuery: Kenmore LG washer LE error locked motor fix

faq.q: Why does unplugging for 30 minutes help?
faq.a: The motor has a thermal overload protector that trips when the motor overheats. Unplugging for 30 minutes allows the motor to cool and the protector to reset.

faq.q: Can the LE error be caused by a simple overload?
faq.a: Yes. Overloading is the most common cause. Reduce the load and try again after the 30-minute reset.

faq.q: What does a bad rotor look like?
faq.a: A failing rotor will have magnets that are cracked, loose, or shifted out of position. These magnets should be evenly spaced and firmly attached.

title: Water Level Sensor Error
shortDescription: The water level pressure sensor is not providing valid readings.
fullDescription: Error code PE on LG-manufactured Kenmore washers indicates the water level pressure sensor (frequency sensor) is not working properly. The sensor monitors water level by measuring water pressure, and the control board has detected readings that are out of the expected range.
symptoms: PE displayed on control panel
Washer may overfill or underfill
Cycle stops or will not start
Water level appears incorrect

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer for 10 minutes. Air bubbles in the pressure hose can cause false readings that may clear with a reset.

fixSteps.title: Check pressure hose
fixSteps.instruction: Access the pressure sensor (usually on top of the tub). Check the small hose connecting the sensor to the tub for kinks, cracks, or disconnection. Clear any blockages.

fixSteps.title: Test pressure sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor. Using a multimeter, check the sensor's electrical output. Blow into the hose port and verify the readings change.

fixSteps.title: Replace pressure sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor does not respond properly to pressure changes, replace it. Transfer the pressure hose to the new sensor.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor and hose test good, the control board may need professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Kenmore LG washer PE error water level sensor fix

faq.q: What type of sensor does LG-made Kenmore use?
faq.a: LG-manufactured washers use a frequency-based pressure sensor rather than a traditional analog pressure switch. It sends a frequency signal that varies with water pressure.

faq.q: Can soap buildup cause PE?
faq.a: Yes. Soap residue can build up in the pressure hose over time, partially blocking it and causing inaccurate readings.

faq.q: Is PE a common error?
faq.a: PE is moderately common. The pressure sensor is an affordable part and relatively easy to replace.
